prompt: Write four absurd but coherent sentences in the voice of a confused medieval chronicler who has been hired to document the Q3 results of a modern SaaS company.
response:
The quarter began with much ceremony, as the Chief Revenue Officer, a man of most solemn countenance, declared we had "exceeded our forecast" — a strange portent I do not fully comprehend.

Our Customer Success team, once known as "kings of retention," now speaks in tongues of "NPS" and "churn," counting not crowns but "points" and "leavers" with great agitation.

The Product team, locked away in their tower of glass, hath wrought wonders upon the "UI" and "UX," though I confess I know not what these sorceries mean, save that the users seem pleased.

The Board of Directors, a council of most serious mien, hath spoken in numbers that make my head spin: "23% YoY growth" and "92% gross margin" — words that sound like a prophecy from the future, yet they speak of the present as if it were some other land entirely.
